unsupportedGaps: Webhook side effects need sandbox provider keys before execution.

test_cards.title: Authenticated user can complete the primary workflow without hidden console failures
test_cards.goal: Prove the app's highest-value workflow works from first page load through final confirmation.
steps: Open dashboard from a clean browser context.
Create a new record with valid boundary data.
Verify confirmation, persisted state, and reload behavior.
test_cards.acceptanceCriteria: The record exists after reload and no console error or failed API request appears during the flow.

test_cards.title: Role boundary prevents client user from reaching admin-only repair actions
test_cards.goal: Confirm role separation is enforced at navigation, page load, and API mutation layers.
steps: Attempt direct navigation to admin repair task URL.
Attempt the backing mutation request from the browser context.
Capture server response and visible UI state.
test_cards.acceptanceCriteria: Client role receives a blocked state and cannot mutate or view admin-only repair tasks.

test_cards.title: Slow API response preserves form input and shows a recoverable state
test_cards.goal: Expose the common launch bug where slow services erase user work or double-submit forms.
steps: Verify disabled state, spinner, and no duplicate request.
Refresh after completion and confirm saved data.
test_cards.acceptanceCriteria: Only one mutation fires, user input remains visible, and the saved state survives reload.

test_cards.title: Mobile audit report is readable without horizontal overflow
test_cards.goal: Verify the client-ready report can be reviewed on mobile without clipped tables or overlapping action buttons.
steps: Inspect issue table, evidence gallery, and repair task cards.
dataNeeds: Generated audit report with at least two findings
test_cards.acceptanceCriteria: No primary content overflows the viewport and all action labels remain readable.

test_cards.title: Webhook/payment side effects are declared instead of silently skipped
test_cards.goal: Prevent fake confidence by forcing unsupported third-party checks into the audit report.
steps: Inspect env and integration code references.
Mark affected checks as blocked with exact reason.
dataNeeds: Sandbox provider keys or explicit skip approval
test_cards.acceptanceCriteria: The report names the blocked integration checks and does not count them as passed.

findings.title: Client role can load admin repair task detail by direct URL
findings.summary: Navigation hides the admin action, but the route-level loader returns repair task details for a client-role session.

findings.title: Mobile report table overflows 390px viewport
findings.summary: The evidence table uses fixed columns and pushes primary actions outside the viewport.

findings.title: Webhook checks blocked by missing sandbox keys
findings.summary: Payment/webhook confidence cannot be claimed until sandbox credentials are present or the check is explicitly waived.

repair_tasks.title: Enforce server-side role guard on repair task detail route
repair_tasks.why_it_matters: The UI hides admin actions, but direct navigation still exposes admin-only repair task details to a client user.
reproduction_steps: Login with captured client-role browser state.
Observe repair task title, likely files, and agent prompt in the rendered page.
repair_tasks.expected_behavior: Client role should receive a 403 page or redirect and API detail route should return 403.
repair_tasks.agent_prompt: Patch the repair task detail page and API route so authorization is enforced server-side. Keep admin access unchanged, return 403 for client users, and add a Playwright regression for direct URL access.

repair_tasks.title: Make audit report evidence table responsive
repair_tasks.why_it_matters: The launch audit is supposed to be client-ready, but the mobile report currently clips evidence and action labels.
reproduction_steps: Observe horizontal overflow and clipped action column.
repair_tasks.expected_behavior: Evidence rows collapse into readable stacked rows on narrow screens with no body overflow.
repair_tasks.agent_prompt: Refactor the audit evidence table responsive layout so mobile uses stacked row cards while desktop preserves the dense table. Verify no horizontal body overflow at 390px.

runner_tools.name: qa.inspect_repo
runner_tools.description: Inspect package metadata, scripts, route files, API handlers, env expectations, and existing test tooling.
runner_tools.outputShape: { framework, package_manager, scripts, route_count, api_route_count, env_keys }

runner_tools.name: qa.detect_app
runner_tools.description: Detect app stack and support tier so the audit report names first-class and generic coverage honestly.

runner_tools.name: qa.capture_auth_state
runner_tools.description: Open a controlled browser session and store encrypted local Playwright storage state without sending credentials to the web app.

runner_tools.name: qa.start_or_verify_app
runner_tools.description: Run or verify the local app URL, then collect console, network, and route reachability facts.

runner_tools.name: qa.run_test_card
runner_tools.description: Execute an approved test card with Playwright and persist trace, screenshot, video, and structured assertions.
runner_tools.inputShape: { campaign_id: string, test_card_id: string, storage_state_ref?: string }

runner_tools.name: qa.collect_artifacts
runner_tools.description: Normalize traces, screenshots, videos, network logs, and accessibility snapshots into report-ready artifact references.

runner_tools.name: qa.sync_campaign_results
runner_tools.description: Send sanitized campaign results to the web app under one campaign id.

competitive_scorecard.testspriteBaseline: URL, PRD, generated test plan, and black-box crawl are the public baseline.
competitive_scorecard.fusionAdvantage: Local MCP runner inspects repo structure, scripts, routes, API handlers, env expectations, and test tooling before planning.
competitive_scorecard.proofGate: Campaign cannot mark context complete until repo summary and runtime summary sync under one campaign ID.

competitive_scorecard.testspriteBaseline: Generic AI-generated happy-path and broad E2E coverage.
competitive_scorecard.fusionAdvantage: Each card includes risk, data needs, evidence gates, acceptance criteria, and blocked-gap policy.
competitive_scorecard.proofGate: Every generated card must include goal, steps, expected evidence, data needs, and acceptance criteria.

competitive_scorecard.testspriteBaseline: Reports failure and may suggest a fix.
competitive_scorecard.fusionAdvantage: Creates coding-agent repair packets with likely files, exact repro, expected behavior, verification command, and agent prompt.
competitive_scorecard.proofGate: Product bugs are not complete until a repair task can be handed to Codex, Claude, Cursor, or a local model.

competitive_scorecard.fusionAdvantage: Treats unsupported integrations and missing sandbox keys as blocked findings, never as implicit passes.
competitive_scorecard.proofGate: Audit score must include blocked checks and unsupported gaps as first-class report items.

competitive_scorecard.testspriteBaseline: Vendor model behavior is hidden behind the product.
competitive_scorecard.fusionAdvantage: Planned category router can send planning, repo analysis, test generation, repair packets, and patching to different hosted or local models.
competitive_scorecard.proofGate: ModelTask interface and provider adapters must support OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter, Genspark bridge, and Docker-hosted OpenAI-compatible endpoints.

competitive_scorecard.fusionAdvantage: Vercel static/serverless app plus local runner keeps private code local while web reporting stays shareable.
competitive_scorecard.proofGate: Static app loads without framework dev server; API accepts runner sync; artifacts can move to Blob and campaign state to Postgres.
